verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase. Like other Src-family members it is dually acylated (myristoylated at Gly2 and palmitoylated at Cys3), anchoring it to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane. It has no extracellular domain, no transmembrane segment, and no GPI anchor. Its SH2, SH3, and kinase domains all face the cytoplasm. Before committing to 'no': (1) cell_state_induced — no published evidence of stress-induced surface exposure of the LYN protein body; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — not applicable, anchoring is inner-leaflet regardless of tissue; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — inner-leaflet PM pool exists but not extracellular; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no evidence of wash-resistant extracellular anchoring. No antibody/ADC/CAR-T program targets LYN on the cell surface. All biological evidence places LYN on the cytoplasmic face of the PM.
